site.name: Govardhan Reddy Narala
site.headline: Data Scientist and Azure .NET backend engineer building applied ML, analytics, and cloud-native systems.
site.heroIntro: I build production-grade data products, ML prototypes, and backend systems that move from research to reliable delivery.
heroSummary: Master's in Data Science with prior enterprise backend and integration engineering experience across Azure, .NET, SQL, observability, and data-intensive services.
This portfolio is built around case studies: research rigor, system design tradeoffs, and the measurable outcomes behind the work.

profile.headline: Data Scientist | Azure .NET Backend Engineer | Data Engineer
about: I build data-intensive systems that connect applied machine learning, analytics, and production software delivery. My background combines enterprise backend engineering with graduate-level data science work across healthcare AI, graph learning, crime analytics, hunger forecasting, and multimodal modeling.

Before my M.S. in Data Science at the University of Houston-Clear Lake, I worked across Azure, .NET, distributed integrations, observability, and operational analytics in high-availability enterprise environments. I care about production reliability as much as model quality.

highlights: Engineered cloud-native backend services in C#/.NET Core, ASP.NET, and Azure for enterprise workflows spanning 10+ global airline partners.
Built ingestion and orchestration pipelines with Azure Data Factory and Durable Functions, reducing manual processing effort by 30% across production workflows.
Designed SQL analytical layers for self-service bag drop systems, achieving 99.9% data integrity and reducing manual audit effort by 35%.
Optimized SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and Cosmos DB workloads through indexing and query tuning, reducing average query latency by 45%.
Deployed Application Insights, KQL dashboards, and Docker-based Azure DevOps CI/CD improvements, reducing incident triage time by 35% and improving release consistency.
Architected high-availability Dell Boomi integrations across Salesforce, NetSuite, on-prem systems, and 3 fulfillment partners.
Supported 10M+ daily transactions across distributed runtimes while sustaining 99.9% SLA uptime for business-critical workflows.
Engineered event-driven APIs, listeners, and asynchronous workflows to enable real-time synchronization across internal and partner systems.
Implemented validation and control checks across 6 ingestion pipelines, improving data consistency and reducing downstream failures.
Deployed Splunk and AppDynamics observability across 12 distributed services, reducing incident triage time by 40% and cutting MTTR by 2+ hours.
Mentored 60+ students in Databases, Big Data (Hadoop/Spark), and Python, improving assignment accuracy and conceptual understanding across SQL, data pipelines, and analytics workflows.
Assessed 1000+ student assignments and provided actionable feedback to strengthen understanding and academic growth.
Designed and delivered hands-on lab walkthroughs on joins, normalization, query optimization, and data processing, improving student problem-solving speed.
Supported structured office hours for real-time SQL and Python debugging, reducing blockers and improving assignment turnaround time.

education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science

projects.title: CivicQuest
projects.summary: AI-supported community engagement platform for volunteering discovery, sustainability learning, and guided interaction.
highlights: AI-powered community engagement platform
Volunteer discovery and registration journeys
Gamified sustainability learning plus Azure bot integration

projects.title: Disease Networks for Disease Clustering
projects.summary: End-to-end graph learning pipeline that combines disease-gene networks with Gene Ontology features to improve unsupervised biomedical disease clustering.
highlights: Built disease x gene and disease x GO feature matrices to combine graph topology with biological process semantics.
Trained a 3-layer GCN encoder and clustered learned latent embeddings with KMeans for stronger disease-group separation.
Improved inter-vs-intra cluster distance separation from 0.90 to 1.45, 0.85 to 1.25, and 1.10 to 1.85 across 40, 50, and 60-disease subsets.

projects.title: Houston Crime Type Classification (2010-2024)
projects.summary: Large-scale crime analytics pipeline on roughly 3.6M Houston crime records to predict NIBRS offense categories under a realistic temporal holdout design.
highlights: Standardized and merged three differently formatted source datasets into one modeling-ready schema spanning 2010 to 2024.
Engineered Beat, Premise, Month, Weekday, and Hour Group features and cleaned multi-label crime records into separate observations.
Selected MLPClassifier as the best model, reaching 29.37% accuracy and 0.5895 macro AUC on the 2024 holdout set.

projects.title: Multimodal Book Genre Classification and Clustering
projects.summary: Multimodal content-intelligence pipeline that combines book titles and cover images to classify and segment books across large catalog datasets.
highlights: Built a dual-modality workflow using Word2Vec and LSTM for titles plus CNN-based visual features for covers across Book32 and BookCover30.
Performed large-scale preprocessing and missing-data analysis before classification and clustering.
Validated genre separation with KMeans and PCA, surfacing interpretable clusters such as Self-Help, Religion and Spirituality, and Education.

projects.title: Zero Hunger Big Data Analytics Pipeline
projects.summary: Big-data pipeline aligned to UN SDG 2 that processes 86,657 records across hunger, agriculture, and socio-economic indicators into scalable analytics and forecasting workflows.
highlights: Designed ingestion, transformation, and querying workflows across HDFS, Hadoop Streaming MapReduce, Spark, and Hive.
Normalized public data sources including FAO, World Bank, and UNICEF-style datasets into analysis-ready tables for hunger hotspot detection.
Built forecasting and visualization workflows, with project materials reporting about 95% prediction accuracy for hunger-trend evaluation.

projects.title: Adversarial Attacks on Deep Neural Networks
projects.summary: Medical imaging robustness benchmark evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses across four CNN architectures on the MURA X-ray dataset.
highlights: Benchmarked four CNN architectures on 40,561 MURA X-ray images across multiple perturbation levels using FGSM, PGD, and Carlini-Wagner attacks.
Built a preprocessing workflow with resizing, normalization, CLAHE enhancement, bone windowing, and smart cropping for clinically consistent inputs.
Implemented FGSM adversarial training with 50% clean and 50% adversarial batches over 50 epochs, improving FGSM robustness and yielding moderate PGD gains.

projects.title: ASP.NET Core Docs (Fork)
projects.summary: Reference fork of ASP.NET Core documentation used for study and internal notes.
highlights: Maintained as a working reference repo
Useful for .NET architecture and API patterns
Supports learning and implementation review

projects.title: Boomi CI/CD Experiments
projects.summary: Enterprise integration and reliability work around Boomi-based distributed workflows, runtime observability, and support automation.
highlights: Supported 10M+ daily transactions across distributed runtimes while sustaining 99.9% SLA uptime for business-critical workflows.
Implemented validation and control checks across 6 ingestion pipelines to reduce downstream failures and improve data consistency.
Automated health checks, restart workflows, and runbooks to eliminate recurring failures and reduce onboarding time for new engineers.

projects.title: deploy.github.io
projects.summary: Minimal static deployment experiment for lightweight hosting and GitHub Pages-style publication.
highlights: Lightweight static deployment setup
Useful baseline for simple hosting workflows
Small experiment in publishing and delivery

projects.title: Microservices Using .NET Core
projects.summary: Clean-architecture microservices system using messaging, RPC, caching, and multiple persistence layers.
highlights: Designed loosely coupled services with clean architecture
Used RabbitMQ and gRPC for inter-service communication
Applied cache and polyglot persistence patterns

projects.title: Node E-Commerce Use Case
projects.summary: API-oriented e-commerce use case covering shopping flows, backend logic, and service design basics.
highlights: Implemented core e-commerce backend flows
Used API-first design for shopping interactions
Built a foundation for larger full-stack demos
